issues.claim: Analog could not equally come from a different verse
issues.objection: Verse 4.26 has a specific argument-shape (Some offer the senses (hearing and others) in the fires of restraint; others offer the sense-objects…) that the engineering analog tracks. The analog would not equally fit 4.18 (action-in-inaction), 4.7 (avatar doctrine), or 2.47 (right-to-action / fruit-detachment). The verse-specificity is observable in: (a) the central Sanskrit term carried into the engineering body, (b) the falsifiability field which forecloses misreadings specific to THIS verse, (c) the implication which is the operational shadow of THIS verse's specific claim.

notes: Force-fit test for 4.26: swap the engineering layer onto a different verse — does it still fit? No. The analog is verse-specific. PASS.
